terrain_challenges: Well-defined trails but multiple trail junctions (3 trails near summit area) requiring a guide to stay on the correct path
Forest section on the summit climb adds a sense of thrill
From the shrine, the climb to the summit is challenging
Daily ascents and descents, mostly gradual and steady
Summit climb on Day 2 is the main strenuous section
altitude_or_weather_risks: Rapid altitude gain (4,652 ft to 9,915 ft) over 2 days makes trekkers prone to Acute Mountain Sickness (AMS) similar to longer Himalayan treks
At altitudes above 10,000 ft, AMS risk is real and can affect first-time and experienced trekkers alike
Even fit trekkers climbing too high too soon run equal risk of AMS
Sustained rainfall can flood trails or make them slushy and unsafe to walk on
Sustained snowfall can obstruct existing paths, requiring fresh tracks to be cut in snow

fitness_benchmark: Walk, jog, or run 5 km in under 35 minutes (minimum recommended fitness level)
Build cardiovascular endurance and muscle strength
Train core, calves, glutes, quadriceps, and hamstrings
approval_requirements: Screenshots must show consistency and meeting trek fitness criteria
Runs must meet trek fitness criteria at least 3 times in the past month
prep_window: 15 to 20 days before trek date for screenshot upload
Runs must meet criteria at least 3 times in the past month

experience_themes: Autumn: post-monsoon clear skies, brown/orange leaves, crisp pine-scented air
Winter: snow experience, camping and trekking in snow
Spring: melting snow at upper reaches, green grass, tiny flowers, rhododendron bloom
Summer: green colour palette, coniferous and oak trees, bright sunny forests and meadows
Post-monsoon clear skies washed clean of haze
Autumn: orange/brown colour palette from leaves
primary_watchouts: Well-defined trails but multiple trail junctions (3 trails near summit area) requiring a guide to stay on the correct path
Forest section on the summit climb adds a sense of thrill
From the shrine, the climb to the summit is challenging
Daily ascents and descents, mostly gradual and steady
Summit climb on Day 2 is the main strenuous section
Total ~14.5 km over two trekking days with ~5,263 ft altitude gain (4,652 ft to 9,915 ft)
best_for: Snow experience and camping in snow (December-February)
Autumn scenery with clear skies and orange/brown foliage (September-November)
Spring blooms including rhododendrons and wildflowers (mid-March to April)
Easy trek and family/children introduction to the Himalayas (May-June)
